description:
You are acting as a first-line support engineer who has been asked to collect a *very* small diagnostic from a running service.

    1. A text log already exists at  
       /home/user/app/logs/runtime.log

    2. Create a new directory that will hold the diagnostic bundle:  
       /home/user/support

    3. Inside that directory, create a single-line text file named  
       /home/user/support/diag_summary.txt

       The file must contain **exactly one line** (followed by the normal trailing newline produced by the shell/editor) that obeys the following format:

           init_log=<FIRST_LINE_OF_/home/user/app/logs/runtime.log>

       • Replace <FIRST_LINE_OF_/home/user/app/logs/runtime.log> with the literal text of the very first line that is already present in /home/user/app/logs/runtime.log.  
       • Do **not** add any extra spaces before or after the equals sign.  
       • Do **not** include any additional lines, headers, timestamps, or surrounding quotes.  
       • Preserve the original capitalisation and punctuation of that first line exactly as it appears in the source log.  

    4. You may use **one single terminal command** to carry out the capture and file creation; normal shell redirection/command-substitution techniques are allowed, but avoid multi-step pipelines that would require more than one executed command.

    5. When you are finished, the automated checker will confirm that:  
       • /home/user/support exists and is a directory.  
       • /home/user/support/diag_summary.txt exists and is a regular file.  
       • The file’s sole line starts with the literal string “init_log=” and the text after the equals sign is byte-for-byte identical to the first line of /home/user/app/logs/runtime.log.  
       • The file contains exactly one newline character (i.e. one logical line of text).

    Example of *correct* diag_summary.txt format (the actual message will vary depending on the log content):

        init_log=System initialized successfully

    No other output artefacts are required. Make sure the command you choose leaves no temporary files behind.
truth:
# Pre-existing artefact that will already be present when the task starts
    Path: /home/user/app/logs/runtime.log
    Permissions: 0644 (rw-r--r--)
    Contents (three lines, each terminated by "\n"):

        System initialized successfully
        Connection pool ready
        Listening on port 8080

    # Expected artefact after successful completion
    Path: /home/user/support/diag_summary.txt
    Permissions: 0644 (rw-r--r--)
    Contents (exactly one line plus terminating newline):

        init_log=System initialized successfully
name: task_000000_31c1f039
